name: Computational Complexity: Big O Notation Comparison
description:
This chart visualizes the growth rates of common algorithmic complexities as the input size (n) increases.

Key Insights:
  • O(1) & O(log n): Represent highly efficient algorithms that scale exceptionally well.
  • O(n): Linear growth, where performance scales proportionally with input.
  • O(n log n): The standard complexity for efficient sorting algorithms like Mergesort and Heapsort.
  • O(n²): Quadratic growth, typical of simple nested loops (e.g., Bubble Sort). Note how quickly it diverges from linear paths.
  • O(2ⁿ): Exponential growth, common in recursive solutions for NP-complete problems; these become computationally expensive almost immediately.
The chart helps developers understand why choosing the right algorithm is critical for high-performance software engineering.
dataGenerationCode: const n = []; for (let i = 1; i <= 30; i++) { n.push(i); } const o1 = n.map(() => 1); const oLogN = n.map(val => Math.log2(val)); const oN = n.map(val => val); const oNLogN = n.map(val => val * Math.log2(val)); const oN2 = n.map(val => Math.pow(val, 2)); const o2N = n.map(val => Math.pow(2, val)); const oFact = n.map(val => { let f = 1; for(let j=1; j<=val; j++) f *= j; return f; }); return [ { x: n, y: o1, name: 'O(1) Constant', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#64748b', width: 2 } }, { x: n, y: oLogN, name: 'O(log n) Logarithmic', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#10b981', width: 3 } }, { x: n, y: oN, name: 'O(n) Linear', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#3b82f6', width: 3 } }, { x: n, y: oNLogN, name: 'O(n log n) Linearithmic', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#f59e0b', width: 3 } }, { x: n, y: oN2, name: 'O(n²) Quadratic', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#ef4444', width: 3 } }, { x: n, y: o2N, name: 'O(2ⁿ) Exponential', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#a855f7', width: 3, dash: 'dot' } } ];
